source_evidence: Appendix B.7, Eq. (37)-(41): rod = finite cylinder with endcaps. 'The segment collides with the rod if any of the following 4 situations occur: collides with finite cylinder's surface; collides with endcap A (disc); collides with endcap B (disc); or is fully contained within the rod.' Surface check adds 0 <= w.n <= n.n and 0 <= (v+t_w d).n <= n.n (Eq. 37).
purpose: Tests whether a truss member (segment) intersects a finite cylinder (rod) restriction primitive, combining the infinite-cylinder surface test bounded by endcaps, two disc endcap tests, and a containment test.
assumptions: Rod is a finite cylinder with endcaps A and B
Combination of the infinite cylinder and disc primitives with minor modifications
Collision if any of 4 situations occur (finite surface, endcap A, endcap B, fully contained)
For containment, P must be inside the rod, requiring 0 <= v.n/n.n <= 1 to verify P is between the endcaps
formula_or_algorithm: 1) Finite-cylinder surface: use the infinite-cylinder quadratic (Eq. 34-35); a root t_w in [0,1] counts only if its intersection point lies between endcaps: 0 <= w.n <= n.n and 0 <= (v + t_w d).n <= n.n. 2) Endcap A: disc test (Eq. 30). 3) Endcap B: analogous disc test (Eq. 38) with (v + (n.n - v.n)/(d.n) n)-based expression and 0 <= t_w = (n.n - v.n)/(d.n) <= 1. 4) Containment: distance from P to axis <= r (Eq. 40) plus 0 <= v.n/n.n <= 1 (Eq. 41). Collision if any of the four holds.
validation_opportunities: unit tests for each of the four collision situations independently
segment entering through the curved wall vs through an endcap
benchmark on cup/spider problem (rod restriction primitive)

reuse_risk.explanation: Composite of several primitives; correctness depends on properly bounding the infinite-cylinder roots to the endcaps and combining four sub-tests without gaps or double counting.

missing_information: Precise code-level combination of the four sub-tests
Degenerate cases shared with cylinder/disc primitives
